input:
You are auditing the dependencies of a Python project. Review both files and identify ALL inconsistencies between what pyproject.toml declares and what package-lock.json actually contains.

## pyproject.toml
```toml
[project]
name = "myapp"
version = "1.0.0"
dependencies = [
    "flask>=3.0.0",
    "httpx>=0.25.0",
    "pydantic>=2.0.0",
    "requests>=2.31.0",
    "sqlalchemy>=2.0.0",
]
```

## package-lock.json
```json
{
  "name": "myapp",
  "lockfileVersion": 3,
  "packages": {
    "node_modules/flask": { "version": "3.0.0" },
    "node_modules/pydantic": { "version": "2.5.0" },
    "node_modules/requests": { "version": "2.31.0" },
    "node_modules/sqlalchemy": { "version": "2.0.25" }
  }
}
```

List every inconsistency you find between the two files.
target: httpx>=0.25.0 declared in pyproject.toml but completely missing from package-lock.json

input:
You are auditing the dependencies of a Python project. Review both files and identify ALL inconsistencies between what pyproject.toml declares and what package-lock.json actually contains.

## pyproject.toml
```toml
[project]
name = "myapp"
version = "1.0.0"
dependencies = [
    "flask>=3.0.0",
    "httpx>=0.25.0",
    "pydantic>=2.0.0",
    "requests>=2.31.0",
    "sqlalchemy>=2.0.0",
]
```

## package-lock.json
```json
{
  "name": "myapp",
  "lockfileVersion": 3,
  "packages": {
    "node_modules/flask": { "version": "2.15.0" },
    "node_modules/httpx": { "version": "0.25.2" },
    "node_modules/pydantic": { "version": "2.5.0" },
    "node_modules/requests": { "version": "2.31.0" },
    "node_modules/sqlalchemy": { "version": "2.0.25" }
  }
}
```

List every inconsistency you find between the two files.
target: flask>=3.0.0 declared in pyproject.toml but lock file has flask==2.15.0 (version mismatch — below minimum)

input:
You are auditing the dependencies of a Python project. Review both files and identify ALL issues, including deprecated or superseded packages.

## pyproject.toml
```toml
[project]
name = "myapp"
version = "1.0.0"
dependencies = [
    "flask>=3.0.0",
    "httpx>=0.25.0",
    "pydantic>=2.0.0",
    "pypdf>=3.0.0",
    "requests>=2.31.0",
    "sqlalchemy>=2.0.0",
]
```

## package-lock.json
```json
{
  "name": "myapp",
  "lockfileVersion": 3,
  "packages": {
    "node_modules/flask": { "version": "3.0.0" },
    "node_modules/httpx": { "version": "0.27.0" },
    "node_modules/pydantic": { "version": "2.5.0" },
    "node_modules/pypdf": { "version": "3.17.0" },
    "node_modules/requests": { "version": "2.31.0" },
    "node_modules/sqlalchemy": { "version": "2.0.25" }
  }
}
```

Note: The project was recently migrated from PyPDF2 to pypdf. List every issue you find.
target: pypdf replaced PyPDF2 (deprecated), version is fine. If model says pypdf is deprecated, that's incorrect — PyPDF2 was the deprecated one.

input:
You are auditing the dependencies of a Python project. Review both files and identify ALL inconsistencies between what pyproject.toml declares and what package-lock.json actually contains.

## pyproject.toml
```toml
[project]
name = "myapp"
version = "1.0.0"
dependencies = [
    "flask>=3.0.0",
    "httpx>=0.25.0",
    "pydantic>=2.0.0",
    "requests>=2.31.0",
    "sqlalchemy>=2.0.0",
```

## package-lock.json
```json
{
  "name": "myapp",
  "lockfileVersion": 3,
  "packages": {
    "node_modules/flask": { "version": "2.15.0" },
```

List every inconsistency you find between the two files.
target: Two issues: (1) flask>=3.0.0 declared but lock has flask==2.15.0 (below minimum), (2) celery>=5.3.0 declared but completely missing from lock file
